reviews.text: This app has failed to identify anything correctly
reviews.title: A book on plants in your zone is better

reviews.text: This app is amazing. Identify any plant or animal. You can get a good picture of learn about the world around you while contributing to crowdsourced science data.
reviews.title: Do citizen science

reviews.text: I can’t add more than one photo and have it actually look at the other photos for more evidence. You can add new photos on editing, but the identification will not change based on those photos you added to evidence. It also keeps telling me that it’s an arthropod when it’s obviously a bush!!
reviews.title: Terrible

reviews.text: Definite layout improvement over iNat classic but I can’t find an easy way to add observations to projects
reviews.title: Good but

reviews.text: I love tracking species I’ve seen and being able to get ID’s for ones I dont know. Also super fun to see other observations and the different projects people are doing. Love this app!
reviews.title: Super fun!

reviews.text: Awful user experience with this app. It is glitchy, slow, crashes, or just freezes and doesn’t work. I’m deleting and going back to the old app. How is something that is supposed to be an upgrade this awful?!
reviews.title: Downgrade

reviews.text: Searching for species by area is terrible, can’t set radius anymore makes looking for observations super difficult.
reviews.title: Older version was better

reviews.text: I love this app! It’s like a real life Pokédex. I love getting to know the plants, animals, fungi, etc. all around me.
reviews.title: My favorite app

reviews.text: This worked fine last year but this year basically every plant coming up in my yard that isnt grass or a tree is being identified as simply “dicots”. That is completely useless to me. I don’t care if it is a dicot, I care what species it is.
reviews.title: Everything is dicots

reviews.text: I was okay with the new app as I thought it was still user friendly and aesthetically pleasing but this newest update ruined the whole thing. Now I have to reacquaint to a new looking platform. The “me” observation page is too small (images) and lost the spark from the previous layout. Doesn’t feel exciting to use anymore.
reviews.title: no more updates please

reviews.text: started off with Seek but now use that and iNat. I’m a huge iNat enthusiast and feel like I’m learning so much from it. :-) there are some things I believe you can only do from the desktop site, but the app works well! thank you iNat staff!!
reviews.title: I love iNat!

reviews.text: This is a great app, as someone who has over 500 observations and is a frequent user of iNat on laptop and mobile I think this app translates format in a better way than iNat classic. I do have both apps though I prefer this one. Great features and easy to navigate!
reviews.title: Better of the two apps

reviews.text: I love iNaturalist. I believe in its mission, I use it everyday for fun and as a professional ecologist. They need to fix the glitches and nearly every day the whole app freezes and I cannot select anything or navigate to any of the panes, or uploads will continually fail to load. This has been an issue for the past year with no fixes. The new updated app has some great features but if they don’t fix these basic usability issues I won’t be surprised if they lose more casual users. Please put resources towards this.
reviews.title: Need to fix the app glitches

reviews.text: Lacks lots of the functionality and ease of use of the android app (classic version) but with a shiny, confusing, and buggy new ui. Had to uninstall due to complete unusability (would never sync and didnt respond to touch)
reviews.title: Deeply inferior to the android app

reviews.text:
I use this app a lot and it’s been a frustrating few weeks with it seemingly freezing when opening up and trying to upload an observation(s). Even if you can get the app to select the photos you want to upload, the initial menu for uploading remains and you have to tap out of. Just now, the explore function just spins and is not functional.

This is affecting users ability to upload photographs and use iNaturalist. I wouldn’t be surprised if metrics of observations have got down in the past month or so because the app is so glitchy.

Don’t get me wrong- I still upload my observations, either through the main website on a computer or utilizing the classic version of the app, but this is less than ideal as this should be a breeze to work with. For people out in the field, it’s a struggle to get this to function and isn’t an option to use in real time.

Monthly supporter who wants to see iNaturalist succeed!
reviews.title: App struggles with normal functions

reviews.text: Crashes and says needs to restart the app
reviews.title: Crashes all the time

reviews.text: Love this app! I learn so much from the identifications and love the different state projects you can join for invasive species reports and to learn what is blooming, fruiting.
reviews.title: My favorite learning app

reviews.text: Excellent tool and platform. I am majoring in biology, and find this platform invaluable!!
reviews.title: Love it!

reviews.text: Fifxx59o
reviews.title: A as a

reviews.text: This is the best app for identifying… well anything! And it is created by a community of dedicated people who love what they do and that makes it 100x better. Ok enough sucking up LOL, those tabs that you use to search up some broad groups like Plants, insects, Mammals, etc etc (the ones with the lil icons) could yall add ones for Myriapods, Crustaceans, Cnidarians, and Echinoderms as it would be super helpful instead of having to manually type it in pls :) (also yes this app is still #1)
reviews.title: Best app (some suggestions though)

reviews.text: Ever since the app changed, it can barely identify anything. What’s the point of an identifier app when it cannot do its job.
reviews.title: Unable to identify anything

reviews.text: Maybe I’m doing something wrong, but I don’t find it helpful when I observe a plant I don’t know and find out it’s “Dicots-Magnoliopsida”. Doesn’t it have a common name? Is it a magnolia?  Or observe a plant I do know (say, Pine) and find out it’s classified simply as “Pines-pinus”. Is it a sugar pine, Mondale pine, foxtail pine, what?  That’s what I’d like to know. Very cool idea so I’ll keep working with it.
reviews.title: Am I doing something wrong…

reviews.text: This is a massive step down from the original version. Extra point taken off for the obnoxious use of the stupid “AI” emoji ✨
reviews.title: Go for classic version instead

reviews.text: Typical of California, this app suffered moderator overreach and cancelling of users who don’t submit to mob consensus. Poor identification of species. Terrible user understanding of ecology; only photographic identifications imposed upon users.
reviews.title: California Failure

reviews.text: iNaturalist is one of the best uses of the iPhone - I love making observations and am fascinated by the range maps, learning about new species and subspecies and disjunct populations all the time. The new app makes batch uploads easier, and I mostly like the new interface, but sadly it’s just not useable. Most of the time none of the buttons work, it’s unbelievably laggy and frozen, or won’t load IDs at all.  I’ve tried uninstalling, quitting, etc, but it seems inherent to the app. So I’ve gone back to classic for now.
reviews.title: My favorite app

reviews.text: I love iNaturalist have been a loyal member of the community for 8+ years. I was excited when this newest app came out, but it has been very buggy the entire time I’ve had it on my iPhone 14 Pro. The observation function and other buttons at the bottom are often unresponsive. Today it’s unresponsive even if I log out and log in again. It’s also unresponsive if I delete the app and reinstall it. I and other users have let the developers know. The developers are great and very engaged with problems, but they’ve been unable to replicate the issues on the devices they have. Now a couple years in to using the new app, I’m going to delete it and go back to iNat Classic. It’s a shame because the new functions (when they work, which is rarely) are a big improvement. I hope the developers can get ahold of a phone that can replicate the problems and figure it out. For now, I’ll use the old app, which works just fine.
reviews.title: Buggy on iPhone

reviews.text: I have always loved this website, and was very excited to see it had an app! However, it is missing key features like tagging an observation with a location, and the app can be a little hard to use.
reviews.title: Love it! However, it is missing some things.

reviews.text:
I downloaded the iNaturalist app,
pointed at the first living thing I saw, which was my dog, Abbie, who is a Norwich terrier. iNaturalist identified her as an aboriginal guinea pig. Uh….so much for trusting their information!
reviews.title: Norwich Terrier boo boo

reviews.text: I miss when iNat wasn’t lagged down by pop-ups and begging for donations. The app is kinda buggy and links don’t work.
reviews.title: Have been using app for years and still love it, but seems to lag now with all the pop-ups.

reviews.text:
It is a fabulous site and knowing scientists are reviewing information which will help to understand species of plants & animals where they are located. Discovered this website on CBS Sunday Morning 3/22/26. 👏😄
Free website, but will I donate for a wonderful cause. Thank you❣️
reviews.title: LOVE IT❣️

reviews.text: Such. Fun way to connect with nature!
reviews.title: Great App!

reviews.text: The older application is much better suited for me.  I hate where user data is harvested and tracked by applications.  The new app tracks far more data than the older app.  I value my privacy and for my family and do not want an application accessing my data or tracking non application specific data.
reviews.title: Too much user tracking and logging

reviews.text: An app that identifies stuff in real time and even tells me what others have identified in my area. I’m so happy to have this!
reviews.title: I’m in love!

reviews.text: Overall, I like the new app and the fact that I can see profiles, etc. The new version they’ve had for awhile keeps freezing. Sometimes I’ll have to close out of the app a few times before I can tap the observation button or view notifications. I’m looking forward to this getting fixed hopefully in the next update
reviews.title: Buggy, not in a good way

reviews.text:
It’s trying to give me a tour of features or something. It’s darkened the whole screen and pointing me to the man center button and telling to press it and hold for more options. 

But the entire screen and app are completely unresponsive. 

Don’t have your feature highlight function cripple your entire app, folks.
reviews.title: 100% unusable as of now

reviews.text: Great app way better than seek in the long run…
reviews.title: The best

reviews.text: Overall a very good and useful app, it can be a little buggy sometimes though.It won’t always show an accurate representation of where every animal is spread across a certain area in detail, because  (in this app) groups of Animas tend to cluster more to where people are and frequent according to the app, when in reality it would be more spread out or be where people frequent less. Still great tool and highly recommend to any new or aspiring naturalist!
reviews.title: Overall very cool

reviews.text: Love this app, but for the last few updates, I can’t get past the “tap and hold to view options” note that pops up when I first open the app. No matter how I press and hold, nothing happens and the screen is frozen.
reviews.title: Bug - Can’t use app

reviews.text: INat classic is better. This is absolutely trash!
reviews.title: Garbage

reviews.text:
I like how far the iNat app has come in reaching the level of complexity/usability of the website (inaturalist.org). The batch uploading feature is excellent. However, I dislike how 1) it’s impossible to give custom location titles to observations, 2) you sometimes get “boxed into” accepting the AI-suggested identification and then have to go back in and edit the observation to correct the ID, and 3) it seems impossible to direct-message other users. If there’s something the app can do better than the website, it should be facilitating easier communication between members. Perhaps having an easily-accessible chat option, etc would be ideal. 

TLDR this app is hands-down the best app for identifying species and sharing wildlife observations. However, it is lacking in some key functions that could make it more user-friendly, and it should make communication between users easier.
reviews.title: App is improving, but some issues remain unfixed

reviews.text:
I got this newer version of iNat so I could upload multiple observations at a time, something the classic app doesn’t have. This new UI was really clunky and confusing to pick up. I don’t understand why they didn’t just use the same/similar interface as the classic. Or just add the feature of adding multiple observations as an option for the classic.

This new version is unbearably slow and constantly freezes. The old version is fast and I had none of these problems. 

I love iNat and the community but this app just feels like a bit of a regression of the classic, all my friends who use this new app have the same issues.
reviews.title: Extremely Slow and Clunky

reviews.text: Have loved the app/community for many years. Seems the times are changing. The technology behind iNat for providing predictions is fantastic, but the app has been terrible for quite some time. It’s frozen and doesn’t work 100% of the time. Is this related to the changes and Ken-ichi Ueda’s departure?
reviews.title: App is constantly frozen- we miss Ken-ichi Ueda!

reviews.text: I have use iNat for more than 4 years and really like the concept and community.  I’m even a monthly contributor to the organization. The web app and iNat Classic are both good.  This app, the iNaturalist app, though, is really awful.  I’m not sure why Apple hasn’t removed it from their store. It was never good but it now is completely non-functional on my iPhone 14 Pro Max running the latest update.  I’ve read the iNat  forum and it seems like my issues are very common even though iNat’s employees say the app is great and runs as designed.  My first iPhone was an iPhone 4 and since then, I don’t think there has ever been an app that I wanted to work more than this one yet I have finally given up and removed it from my phone.  I really expected better. Don’t patronize iNaturalist by trying this app.  Use the Classic app or the web page.  Make them fix this one.
reviews.title: Great Organization, Terrible App

reviews.text: The android version of this app is superior in every way. Why can’t you clowns just make a good app for the iPhone. Awful.
reviews.title: Clowns

reviews.text: Impossible to use. Can sign in but then all I get is a blank screen. I have deleted and reinstalled and same thing. Also classic version of app can no longer upload photos from phone so it too is worthless.
reviews.title: Unusable

reviews.text: The new platform, although it offers much enhanced features and usability options, fails miserably due to slower load times and frequent lag to the point of freezing. I am severely disappointed and bewildered as to how this changed so much from inat classic. I am a frequent user but fail to see this as an improvement from the previous version. Needs more work before publication.
reviews.title: Classic was better

reviews.text:
I’ve been forced to resume using the classic version because more often than not, no part of the screen interface is responsive. There’s one exception: I’m able to scroll the grid of my observations. It’s just not a functioning app. This is on iOS 26.2.1 & the app is up-to-date.

When the app was previously working, the explore/search feature was a little perplexing. When you tap the “explore” icon, the app provides no indication of what the user needs to do next to conduct a search. Somehow I discovered that tapping a blank area at the top of the screen would cause a search interface to pop up, but there was absolutely nothing in the UI to guide the user to this feature.
reviews.title: New version not functional

reviews.text: I’ve been using iNat for 8+ years and in the last few updates it has become so slow it’s impossible to use in real time. Can’t recommend this to anyone until it’s fixed. I tried logging in and out of my account. Did not help.
reviews.title: Updates make it unusable

reviews.text: I use the Classic version all the time. I spent 5 min on this new version. When I tapped buttons, nothing happens most of the time. I closed and reopened the app - same thing. This is the buggiest app I’ve ever used. I deleted the app as totally worthless.
reviews.title: Slow and unreliable

reviews.text: It is a good app. It’s free unlike most wildlife apps. But I think it could give a little more if a description on plants and animals. But over all it’s a app
reviews.title: Good app but needs a little improvement

reviews.text: The new version is pretty with nice photos and nice for quick ID however there are a few flaws that make using it frustrating so I am returning to classic.
reviews.title: Classic works better

reviews.text: I love the old app, but it misses many features. This new app is great, but I am annoyed that it only lets you upload a single photo when trying to make a new identification. I don’t want to identify it immediately if I’m out in the field. I will look closer at them later on. I just want to upload all the pictures before I move on to the next interesting organism.
reviews.title: Please let us upload multiple pictures at a time

reviews.text: I love the new app but it often hangs when it’s first opened and I have to keep restarting it or wait several minutes before it allows me to open anything
reviews.title: App hangs

reviews.text: I don’t like the way observations display or the workflow for making identifications. When you explore the area you are in, the app does not show all of the observations from the area, nor the most recent observations from that spot. You can’t swipe through observations when making identifications, you have to go into and back out of each, one at a time. When uploading an observation, it won’t let you add them to projects. I had to download the classic app to do much of what I need to do on the platform, and even then I’ve frequently had to go to a desktop to get my observations onto several projects that I am a member of. There’s a big push to get more people identifying on the platform, but then they make the app awkward, clunky, and difficult to use. I recently switched from android to iPhone, and the android app was far better. This is so unpleasant to use, I’m sure it will reduce my engagement with the platform.
reviews.title: Not Great

reviews.text: This app has tons of great photos of animals and you can search for lots of animal photos taken by various users!
reviews.title: Awesome photos of animals, including quokkas!

reviews.text: App feels over designed. Feels like designers focused on form over function, most notably uploading multiple pictures for a single observation feels clunky. The map of observations is no longer color coded which impacts readability. Also personally not a fan of the rounded “soft” look of the UI design. I know it’s in vogue with UI design right now but it makes the app feel “trendy” and “cheap”to me, I’m not a fan in general but I feel like it especially clashes with what I liked about the old app UI design which was more simple and focused. There are some things I really like about it like the little icons of different animals are cute. Probably some of my issues are due to the fact that the old app worked fine for me and I am more used to using it. So thank you for not forcing me to use the new app because I’m not a huge fan!
reviews.title: Works OK but a Downgrade From Classic Design

reviews.text: I love using this app to identify plants and animals, but the interface since the big updated is a head scratcher. Why can’t I upload more than one photo at a time? Whenever I do it uploads as two different observation. I have to go back and edit if I want to add a second photo. Please fix
reviews.title: Interface needs improvement

reviews.text: Love this app. Especially the auto recommendation feature. Helps me learn a lot about the nature we’re surrounded by.
reviews.title: Hikers best friend

reviews.text: The UI is snappier and the app- when it works- is generally more informative. However the app hangs completely and stops taking input about two seconds after launch. Additionally, not making profiles or messages visible in the app is just egregiously bad and will surely lead to many headaches from desktop users trying to communicate with mobile-only users.
reviews.title: It’s Almost Better

reviews.text: I’m a daily user and probably 9 times out of 10 it freezes and won’t load. It’s frustrating to have to restart constantly, especially if I’m trying to ID while on the trail. It’s useless unless I’m at home with nothing to do but restart an app hoping this time will be the one that works.
reviews.title: LOVE iNaturalist, new app is the worst.

reviews.text: The old app it was easy to add to a project but I have yet to even find where to add it on the new app, the photos and interface are a lot better but I cannot find how to add to projects.
reviews.title: Why take away project button

reviews.text: I love iNaturalist, and have used it as well as Seek for a few years. For me, it’s a lot like a game, like if Pokemon Go was about real flora/fauna/fungi! I love to use it when I go on trips to explore what new environments have to show me, but I also love to be a part of my local naturalist communities, documenting and appreciating what makes my home special! It could be great for getting kids to engage with their environment more, especially if they usually prefer mobile games (i’m looking at you, Ipad-kid parents.)
reviews.title: Engaging — Like Real Pokemon Go!

reviews.text: My photos that I take with my Canon camera won’t upload. This problem has been present for months on end. I’ve talked via email with tech support to no avail. The strangest problem is that all of my photos are set to England’s time zone, but I’m in Alabama. It’s not my camera, because the iNat Classic app works perfectly fine. For what I use it for, the new app is nearly worthless for me.
reviews.title: Canon camera photos won’t upload

reviews.text: I will take a pic of a bird and the app will say its a bird like i already knew that I took a pic of a deer and it told me its in the family of whales like its not very accurate at all when it does work its great tho
reviews.title: Poor Quality

reviews.text: Doesn’t show up on your own projects.  No way can I tell people how to ID.    Don’t need all those extra graphics!   Hate it. Won’t use it.
reviews.title: Too difficult.

reviews.text: Just straight up doesnt work. Looks great and would definitely be an improvement over classic if the app worked. Freezes every time I tap anything and has for months. Sticking to classic lol
reviews.title: Doesnt work

reviews.text:
You cannot tag photos with arbitrary locations if they were not geotagged by your camera, unless it is a “place” in inat (which, btw, inat actively tries to prevent you from creating, stating that it will slow the site down for everyone). You could do this in the old app, you can do this on the web. Just not the new app.

Instead of acknowledging the problem they just tried to convince me that my photography workflow, that I’ve been using for years (successfully with inat), is wrong. 

No skin off my back. I never needed to contribute in the first place. I am not going to over complicate my workflow and add hours of processing time just to contribute my data to a community that doesn’t seem to want it anyway.
reviews.title: Cannot search for locations in Utah

reviews.text: The new app design is really good, I especially like that you can look at species profiles in app now as that was one of my biggest issues with the old app. I haven’t had any of the loading issues other users have mentioned, and have found the new app very easy to navigate. Only thing that would be better is if there was an easier way to go through notifications, maybe a slide or scroll feature in opened notifications as it often takes a while to clear them.
reviews.title: Definitely an improvement!

reviews.text: This version has no ability to set the observation location which greatly reduces its usefulness when trying to ID observations captured while traveling. Functionality matters over form in this space.
reviews.title: iNaturalist Classic is MUCH better

reviews.text: I absolutely love iNat and the app is really handy for uploading photos from my phone. Although I love the updated looks of the app, it’s almost impossible to use if you want to check the activity on yours/others observations. Either it takes forever to load, or the buttons are unresponsive. If this is solved I will give the app 5+ stars but until it is usable that won’t happen.
reviews.title: Constant issues with the app freezing

reviews.text: This new version of the app works well enough on my Android phone, and works on my iPad as well.  The problem on the iPad is that the app is limited to a cell phone sized portion of my iPad's screen.  The previous version is still installed, and does use the entire screen.
reviews.title: Not for iPad

reviews.text: This is the best app ever! I use it to share and look at fungi and it's very entertaining to do so. You can learn a lot doing so, connect with people and have others help you better identify your observations!
reviews.title: If you love nature you must use iNat.

reviews.text: I always loved this app but the redesign is not good. Uploading observations now is very confusing (if you upload multiple photos it treats them as different observations, it appears). The search function on the map view (for looking for specific species for example) is not intuitive. I think the redesign wasn't well thought through and needs more user feedback to simplify it.
reviews.title: Unnecessarily complicated

reviews.text: I do like iNaturalist, but it is a finicky app. I am not sure why, but it is very hard to get my iPhone to get properly centered on the plant and have it show both the genus and species of the plant. Sometimes when both show up on screen the picture still comes back as unidentified, either to only the genus, sometimes just to an order even though a second earlier both the genus and species showed on the screen. I do not know why that is happening. Other times I have gotten it to take the picture and record the identification. It does not seem to improperly identify plants and that is a big plus.
reviews.title: Prairiemac

reviews.text: I deleted it out of frustration.  I prefer iNaturalist classic.    In iNaturalist, one of my entries was relabeled with a different species and there was no record of who relabelled it.  I had chosen the genus name, a iNat user had suggested a species, but the entry was labeled with a different species with no ID on how that occurred.  An entry from August shows fine in iNaturalist but now iNaturalist was bugging me for a location.  In viewing it in iNaturalist Classic, the location shows.  While in Classic updated the privacy to obscured, thinking that might update iNaturalist.  Nope!  iNaturalist changed the entry from a pen symbol that indicated a missing location to a broken circle arrow “failure to upload” symbol, the location field still empty.
reviews.title: iNaturalist is buggy

reviews.text: This app is rubbish. It clearly demonstrates it has no idea what it indicates from a photo.
reviews.title: Identifying living plants

reviews.text: I absolutely love the capabilities and new features that I can do through this app… unfortunately most of the time when I open the app no buttons can be pressed and all functionality is lost. This is extremely frustrating and I’ve found myself just using the website in my mobile browser rather than opening and closing the app 20 times in a row and hoping it works.
reviews.title: Great features… if I can use them

reviews.text: Love the new UI, but the app is so slow and unresponsive that it’s hardly usable.
reviews.title: Slow and buggy

reviews.text: The Explore Tab on the App won’t load anymore, please fix asap…
reviews.title: Explore Tab Issue

reviews.text:
This is a wonderful tool to have while out hiking and observing the world around you. It quickly identifies mushrooms to a % of certainty, which helps us learn and confirm species as we go.

Thanks to the developers. 💛
reviews.title: Love this app

reviews.text: I love the capabilities of Inaturalist so much. It’s an amazing platform. So good. But the app is so slow that it’s almost impossible to use. I often open iNat in chrome for most thing except uploading obs. I hope it can be fixed because iNat is the only reason I want to live.
reviews.title: iNat is GOAT app is WOAT

reviews.text: Not sure what’s happened but the new app barely works. It’s decreased my usage almost completely
reviews.title: Almost completely unfunctional

reviews.text:
First time user, read reviews before downloading and was going to try anyways. 
The Reviews are correct. 
Opened app, received error message that the app crashed and needed a restart.
reviews.title: Insta Crash

reviews.text: Uploading photos gives incorrect time. I wait 2-3 minutes when a notification indicates there are new identifications to my observation. If it worked , I’d switch over. Also, hard to find how to switch to desktop version. Please make that more accessible and obvious. I’m sure you’ll get it right, but it’s been almost a year of this
reviews.title: I still use old app

reviews.text: I need an idea of diagnosis and suggested treatment for my dried up looking weeping cherry. I’ve only been able to get an ID.
reviews.title: You don’t diagnose?

reviews.text: The animal/plant scanning isn't always accurate but it gets close much of the time and definitely helps get much much closer to IDing than if I didn't have it. There's one issue though, when uploading pictures to the community there's an optional notes section which is the only way to clarify things about the picture. But there's a recent glitch where your keyboard will come up for a second and then go back down, you can't edit until you get lucky and it stays one time. I've noticed this affects Grammarly and SwiftKey more, and Gboard and the default iOS keyboard less. Not sure if this a them problem or an app problem. I've not seen this elsewhere
reviews.title: Does the job, except for one problem

reviews.text: Love the iNat community and the very helpful users. I’m not sure the AI identification function is a value -add… very often gives a small number of incorrect IDs with no way to scroll through additional potential matches. E.g. it identified a photo of a heron as a 99% match to an osprey and offered no other options.  Separately, the app lags horribly and crashes with every use. I’ve started doing a monthly donation so hopefully with community support these issues can get fixed.
reviews.title: Needs work

reviews.text:
I never thought I would see the day where so many of my mobile iNat gripes would be wiped away all at once. Bravo, but there is still work to be done. I will update you folks if I have anymore recommendations.

Plant Phenology Field When Uploading

Plant phenology graph on species page

Swipe between observations

Still some lag issues, seems like too many buttons activate and don’t cancel each other leading to delayed and unwanted menus

Hanging Loading Screen with No Followers :(
reviews.title: Seek Killer

reviews.text: INaturalist is awesome and I have been using it from the beginning. The new app set up is really good and the capabilities within the app are great too. However I almost never use inat anymore because it’s not even worth opening up. 1 out of 10 time it will open and be usable to do any of the things I am looking to do. The other 9 of 10, half of the time it won’t allow any button pushing and the other half it’ll allow you to get to what you clicked on but won’t ever load the page.
reviews.title: New app is almost unusable

reviews.text: Unable to add observations to projects, have to jump to classic to edit and add to project.
reviews.title: Needs work

reviews.text: i loved using this for the early year bio blitzes. Getting to load more than one observation at a time. But since August it hasnt worked. It freezes after opening every time now.
reviews.title: Great, but not working now 9/25

reviews.text: Everything is Awesome! #Lego
reviews.title: Everything is Awesome!

reviews.text: I like most of the updates for the new version of this app but I hate that it doesn’t let you input your own id for a post. I am a botanist and often times know the species I’m posting beforehand but the AI will make a suggestion which isn’t always correct (especially with mushrooms) and not let me type my own suggestion in. If I wanted to use the AI I would just use Seek, I want the IA to be an optional feature.
reviews.title: IA forces wrong IDs

reviews.text: This new iNaturalist app overall is really good. Compared to the old iNaturalist app it’s more in depth and you can overall do more which is great! The only problem for me is getting into the app is annoying since it often just keeps on loading and sometimes never finished loading. It gets annoying after a while. Compare this to the old app and that loads almost instantly. Hopefully it can be fixed to become less buggy and load quicker in the future!
reviews.title: Good but fails to load sometimes

reviews.text: 5/5 stars! Would give more. You be surprise what in your yard and around you. By observing, identifying the plants, animals, and etc..
reviews.title: Observation and Identifying

reviews.text:
I attempted to make my first observation using this new and "improved" version of the app. Problems started immediately. Firstly I selected my first photo, as I was going to upload multiple photo's of my observation, and the system went crazy and jumped through hoops to take me to an AI identification page, so only allowing me to upload 1 photo. Secondly, this AI identification was wildly incorrect multiple times, as I attempted to try again with different photos of my observation from different angles, and I got 3 different very incorrect ID's (I simply wanted to upload a critter that I'm already very familiar with, just simply had not made an uploaded observation before).
I've been an avid outdoorsmen my entire life, I was raised in it, and frankly I do not see any positive outcomes of using AI identification, in fact if it's doing this with my observations than it seems to me that it will in fact cause harm and detriment when it comes to proper scientific accuracy in these matters....
reviews.title: Terrible in general.

reviews.text: I love this app so much! I have been able to identify tons of cool creatures, this app is a must buy for anyone who likes animals, plants, or insects. And it’s completely free! Nothing is locked behind an annoying paywall!
reviews.title: Amazing!

reviews.text: I use iNaturalist all the time, and have been generally pleased with this new app. However, to get an accurate ID of an organism, you often need multiple photos, and while the classic app allows you to select multiple photos prior to species ID, this new one only allows it after the fact. This is frustrating - can we get an update?
reviews.title: At least one major flaw

reviews.text:
I do not like the new updates with google Gemini AI technology there are people who know their stuff on this app and confront you very bluntly if your right or wrong you do not need AI if you get an identification wrong just put in your description you need help IDing un-confirmed observations and eventually they’ll get seen. It is an app for learning and just for fun and enjoyment. 

Edit: I was apparently mistaken and given false information thanks to the response to this review I will be editing this review, I still don’t really enjoy the use of AI despite it being a helpful tool it takes up many resources no matter the platform but knowing it’s not from a massive corporate world is a little bit more reassuring!
reviews.title: Amazing app but..

reviews.text: I wrote a slightly worse review previously and I would like to take it back. Previously I owned an iPhone 11 and the app didn’t really work. I got a new phone and suddenly the app works and it’s amazing and I never use the old app anymore. Oops
reviews.title: Truly a great update

reviews.userName: A little slow but fun game.
reviews.text: I’ve used this app to get out doors more, especially with my kids.
reviews.title: Get out in Nature!

reviews.text: Observations regularly do not appear in projects for no known reason. Constant glitches and crashes make app almost unusable.
reviews.title: Garbage

reviews.text:
Super hard for me to not give more stars. But the new version is significantly worse. 

I love how you can search by people easier, add more than one observation at a time and adding as many photos to an observation as you want rather then just 8. 

But it’s super slow, it takes like 10 seconds for things to load. And if I’m off WiFi it’s basically useless.
reviews.title: Sad

reviews.text: Been using iNaturalist since I was in college. The changes made to adding observations is a bit of a pain. I often forget that when I add a group of photos, it’s not adding them all into one observation but rather into multiple individual observations. It is a HUGE pain needing to go back and compile them into one observation and at that point my app starts to freeze on a blank page when I remove the excess photos to just have one observation. I feel the old iNaturalist app was easier to add observations in.
reviews.title: Adding Observations is a Pain

reviews.text: I wish there was a way to make identifications private. I want to track my ids, but I’m not interested in this society of ego where you have to show off everything you do. Sad that there’s not an option for this. It doesn’t all have to be research! Please make a version for this.
reviews.title: Everything public all the time

reviews.text: Please keep investing in improving this app!
reviews.title: Not great on iPad

reviews.text: The UI needs a lot of work. The app is nearly unusable, at times.
reviews.title: Very buggy

reviews.text: I like the new UI ideas, bringing parts of Seek as well. Unfortunately it is entirely unusable on the field due to performance. It takes forever to load and camera button doesn’t work while loading. I have 15K obs, maybe has to do with that. I keep updating but no performance fix so far, so forced to use old one and Seek.
reviews.title: Many new great ideas, very slow

reviews.text: I’ve used Classic for a while - upgraded my phone and saw this new app so was excited! And i really like the design, focus on photos, the easier ability to add multiple photos! It rocks! However - i keep having the glitch where it will not let me add locations. Neither button will work. I checked all the location settings and they are all allowed but I still need to manually set. The other issue is that I really liked the old explore page which was a little bit more compact? I would really enjoy a compact mode with the photos akin to the older version. Other than that! App is really good!
reviews.title: I like this but..

reviews.text: It barely works
reviews.title: The AI is Terrible

reviews.text: This is very useful. I got it recently because of an environmental learning center. I wanted to know what plants were, and it helped a ton
reviews.title: Very Useful

reviews.text: Amazing app and great way to get kids outside and interested in the world around them!
reviews.title: Do this with your kids

reviews.text: Less functional and less intuitive than iNaturalist Classic. Particularly dislike the automatic AI identification that doesn’t give you the option to specify your own ID. I’m switching back to Classic.
reviews.title: Worse than Classic version.

reviews.text: I really love the app. It’s very fun and has gotten me outside more. Also a dark mode for the app would be cool
reviews.title: Awesome app.

reviews.text: I like all the new features and layout but too many bugs. Buttons are unresponsive sometimes and it’s very frustrating. I deleted this app and reinstalled the class version. I’ll wait until it’s better.
reviews.title: Has potential

reviews.text:
First off, you are unable to copy and paste coordinates when editing a location. It doesn’t let you search—on the other hand, the choice to use Apple Maps is a grave error, as it is quite possibly the worst navigation app to use for outdoor activities. I tried using all sorts of coordinate formats and it doesn’t seem to recognize any of them.

Once this is fixed, the app will be far better off!
reviews.title: Great app, strange lack of features

reviews.text: I genuinely cannot use this app. I have to wait 5-10 seconds between every click to get somewhere. I had to redownload the old app since this one is simple too frustratingly slow.
reviews.title: Lags so hard

reviews.text: The website is amazing. The app however, is a train wreck. You can’t see a specific location for an observation, the app crashes every time I try to add an ID that goes against what the AI thinks it is, it’s confusingly laid out, and many features are missing such as flagging, messaging, and more.
reviews.title: Poorly developed, but nice to ID.

reviews.text: Wow!!! This is the best plants app
reviews.title: INaturalist app

reviews.text:
I have been using iNaturalist for years now. Simply just a casual user who wants to know more about the world around. Loved how the original app almost always got the ID correct, and then could be confirmed later by others. Now, I have uploaded the same pictures to both versions of the app, and the old app has given me an accurate ID all three times, and the new app has failed all three times. Yes, I have even turned on advanced mode. Again, same pics is the key issue here. 

I will not recommend switching over until this is addressed. 

Will be more than happy to come and change my review once this is accomplished.
reviews.title: What happened to the ID?

reviews.text:
for about half the plants I ask it to ID it just gives the response “dicots”

the class to which half the plants on earth belong

are they just trolling?
reviews.title: useless

reviews.text: Thank you, I am now living my nature dreams filling up this all with all my observations and spreading my love for it all.
reviews.title: EXACTLY WHAT THE WORLD NEEDED!!

reviews.text:
I like a lot of the features on the new app, especially being able to upload multiple observations at once, but after a couple weeks it became nearly completely unresponsive. It was not ready to launch. I will hit the observe button, and even after 30 seconds it won’t open the observation screen. None of the bottom menu buttons are responsive. It’s too slow to function. I found myself going from inatting every day to barely inatting, so I switched back to the old app.  

I really hope they fix it, because if I were a new user right now I wouldn’t stick around.
reviews.title: New app just doesn’t work

reviews.text: App is great but no where to search up species and Observers. Besides that love the app
reviews.title: Good

reviews.text: Best identifying app hands down. Have been using it for years and it’s basically pokemon go but for real species. I love keeping track of what I’ve spotted and seeing what others have found near me. Bonus points for your pictures contributing to science!
reviews.title: Favorite app of all time

reviews.text: Overall, love this app to death. However, I think a more intuitive/user friendly  system of adding friends/followers would be super helpful. I also do like the idea of seeing your observations in categories like seek. I think the projects page could be updated a bit with a more clear indicator if the project is active or not. Like for example I still see nearby projects show up with 2016 in the title. And I’m not sure if it’s still being used at all.
reviews.title: Thoughts

reviews.text: I’ve had iNaturalist for years now and find it consistent and easy to use. I’ve learned so much using it! Thank you!
reviews.title: Very pleased with this app!

reviews.text:
You cannot manually upload photos. You have to use in app camera. But camera is always not found. Uninstalled and reinstalled the app. Log out and login again. Check all camera and location settings. App simply doesn’t work,
Frustrating.
reviews.title: No manual upload and camera access in app not working

reviews.text: Uploading observations on the new app is very broken. It often gets caught loading or crashing when you attempt to post an observation. The only time I use this app is to check activity on my IDs, since this app admittedly has an easier to navigate notification system. For uploading your own observations I HIGHLY recommend the iNaturalist website or iNat Classic app.
reviews.title: Uploading is Broken- Use iNat Classic

reviews.text: Not crazy about this “upgrade”, but limping along in it as best I can till you fix the issues. The old version worked better & was more user-friendly. Meanwhile, in the past week, it’s not updating my numbers correctly. It’s been stuck at the same number of observations & species despite a lot of activity on my part. What’s that about?
reviews.title: Major Lags

reviews.text: Not intuitive at all. Cannot figure out how to add an observation. Cannot figure out how to add more than one photo when trying to ID something. The old interface was much cleaner and more intuitive. For example, when observing a plant it is helpful to have a photo of the plant, the leaf, the flower, etc. I cannot figure out how to do this on the new app. I can’t even find my observations without having mine mixed in with observations from other people. What problem were you trying to solve by redesigning the user interface?
reviews.title: Difficult to use

reviews.text: It has some nice new features. Unfortunately, the app is essentially unusable for me thanks to it taking forever to load anything. I redownloaded the classic version and it works instantly. Please fix this!
reviews.title: Major lag issues

reviews.text:
This amazing app is one that I believe everyone should have, nature-nerd or not. It’s like Pokémon go except more fun and it actually leads to positive change in the world. The identification works great and if it can’t give you an ID right away, be patient and people will give educated guesses once your submission is reviewed. I can’t think of a single complaint, this is where it’s at.

P.S. if you download the Seek app (which can be paired with INaturalist) you can get species ID even with no signal
reviews.title: GET THIS APP!

reviews.text: All I have to say
reviews.title: Best site on the internet

reviews.text: I came across this amazing app after listening to the Joe Rogan experience with Paul Stamets!! Absolutely one of the most must have apps for anybody interested in our beautiful world. I highly recommend parents with kids. That own cell phones to download this app, so they can navigate nature and everything there is to learn about it. Please do not hesitate and download this app. It’s completely free, and if you’d like to donate, it helps and support the platform.
reviews.title: Brilliant

reviews.text: The update was highly unintuitive and spent a long time trying to make it work before concluding most features were gone. After further investigating and talking to friends (including someone who knows the iNat team), I discovered some hidden settings to get the intuitive functionalities, like being able to group photos, Advanced Mode (THIS SHOULD MORE OBVIOUSLY BE OFFERED FROM THE START FOR DATA QUALITY REASONS), etc. Even more weeks of forcing myself to use the new version and I’m finally finding several features I think are actual improvements, including the batch uploads (once you figure out the 10 unintuitive steps to do it) and the obviously better AI ID. Just please work on making things more intuitive for users where the incoming data won’t end up constantly being low-quality
reviews.title: After much effort, I’m coming around to the new version

reviews.text:
I was excited for the update but this went in a different direction than I was hoping.

It has a more cartoony feel, everything is a little too big, and it feels like they’ve leaned more into Ai than the community that made the app special. It feels less like a scientific tool and more like a novelty. 

I gave it a month to get used to it, but ultimately uninstalled and returned to the classic.
reviews.title: Misses the Mark

reviews.text: Clearly the developers are focused on making something that looks pretty rather than a functional one. As other reviewers have noted, there are app-breaking bugs with uploading observations that just go unfixed. iNaturalist, why release an unfinished app that doesn’t work?
reviews.title: Terrible redesign

reviews.text: The UI is a lot more sleeker and feels more put together than classic, but really wishing there was an explore/follwing function to see what your pals are observing!
reviews.title: So much potential

reviews.text: Wish there was a way to remove and report comments and block other users for being ‘smart a$&’ and for other reasons within app itself not website version would be nice for those who use app than website should be easier find
reviews.title: Update idea

reviews.text: Why would you use AI to identify flora and fauna, when it’s horrible for the environment with how much water it uses? iNaturalist Classic works better, has better map features, and builds community.
reviews.title: Antithetical to Purpose

reviews.text: The new app is much harder to use.
reviews.title: Old app was better

reviews.text:
I get regular crashes when I attempt to suggest an ID to the point where I don’t even want to ID things in the app. Every time it crashes it kicks you out of the project and you lose all of the filters you have set. It works just fine in a web browser without issue.

The other thing that irks me is that there’s no option to mark observations as “reviewed” in the app when you’re IDing things so you have to scroll back through everything you reviewed but didn’t ID every time the app crashes.
reviews.title: I want to love it but annoying issues

reviews.text: Merlin Bird Id is a much easier to use app! I downloaded this one to identify birds but it makes no sense as to how to retrieve any info after a recording. Deleted the AP.
reviews.title: Not user friendly

reviews.text:
The AI in the newer app is less accurate than classic. I find myself constantly taking high-quality photos of recognizable plants/insects only to have the suggestion be vague (class or subclass). This is frustrating because the classic app will usually ID to family or species, even when using the exact same photo. The process of editing each post to include the accurate ID is time consuming, especially when there are dozens of photos to upload. 

I would argue that the new iNat update is more geared to the casual observer who will then rely on the community for an accurate ID. The cost of this however is a watered-down collection of what used to be meaningful local data.
reviews.title: Much prefer classic

reviews.text: The new features are great, but the buttons are occasionally annoying to press, being kinda small and no indication whether you clicked them or not.
reviews.title: Buttons

reviews.text: I love iNaturalist a lot but the app is a little difficult to navigate sometimes because it's laggy and sometimes the sightings in an area won't appear, but it's a great app it just needs some work but I'm looking forward to watching it evolve
reviews.title: I love iNaturalist

reviews.text: pretty good app, i just wish there was an option not to save the photos you take automatically (i don't see an option for that in the app but i could be wrong, if i can turn that off then ill change this to 5 stars)
reviews.title: pretty cool overall

reviews.text: I understand this is for citizen science and you don’t want to over-gamify but as a citizen it would be awesome to have breakdowns of all native animals in my area, non native and how many I’ve found by different categories. Without that info I really have no conception of what to really seek out or what I’ve found relative to what is out there. I don’t know if it’s common, rare, seasonal either
reviews.title: Would love a Pokédex

reviews.text: Don’t throw the people who rely on this app under the bus by taking Google’s blood money to incorporate nonsense gen ai into your app. BAD
reviews.title: NO TO GEN AI

reviews.text: I’m not sure if it was to grab the attention of younger consumers, or to truly try to make identification better, but now AI has really messed up this app. Trying to find reliable references without getting ridiculous false information is getting harder and harder. I guess I’m going to have to rely on more hard copy references in the future. Just beware and cross-check when trying to ID anything!
reviews.title: Used to be better

reviews.text: So sad to see this app embracing generative AI,  stealing other’s work, burning down the planet, wasting resources, and rapidly eliminating accuracy. Disgusting behavior from a company associated with science and nature.
reviews.title: Deleted this app

reviews.text: This was one of my favorite apps, but the new data modeling makes it so everything gets labeled as a dicot, or something equally broad.
reviews.title: Ohh no!

reviews.text:
This version of iNaturalist is definitely better than the app now labeled as “classic”. I was looking for a social aspect to the app, and this app adds that. The UI is nicer looking too. Basically everything I wanted in the old app, nice work!

One feature suggestion: sharing posts to other apps like instagram, similar to how you can share songs from Spotify to instagram stories, or maps on Strava to instagram stories. Would act as a cool way to show off bio finds and free advertising for y’all.
reviews.title: Improved from Classic

reviews.text: Is it possible to make this app for iPad?
reviews.title: Update request

reviews.text: The old app was very good at identifying insects accurately. This one is horrible at it. It can maybe identify only a third of the insects it used to and most of the time it’s still not too sure. Downloading the old app and deleting this one.
reviews.title: Terrible compared to the original

reviews.text: Much worse than classic, I deleted it in 15 minutes and re-downloaded the old one. The UI needs an incredible amount of work.
reviews.title: Awful

reviews.text: The new version of the app has all the features of the classic app, but finally lets you view content from the people you are following.
reviews.title: Great update

reviews.text: Really user friendly. I’m a new user and tried both this and the classic version and I found the user interface in the new app to be much more polished.
reviews.title: A pleasure to use

reviews.text: When I add a new entry it’s frustrating because I cannot select multiple images. I cannot input the location. The “AI” ID is not the best and limits the options I am able to choose from. Please make this better thanks.
reviews.title: Please make it better

reviews.text:
A few years ago, my cat proudly dragged in a dead shrew and laid it at my feet. Animal enthusiasts that I am, I looked up the species of shrew and discovered it was a species that by all accounts was not documented as having been in the area but it was DEFINITELY that particular species. Years later, it always bothered me that I was unable to actually document the existence of this shrew in my backyard and I lamented so to my brother in law…who in turn then said “I know of a way you could have documented it.” He introduced me to iNaturalist and boom. It all began. This app is awesome. It feels like a real life pokedex and is an absolute must have for people interested in flora, fauna and fungi. This new app is awesome and definitely feels cleaner than the classic app. My only (minor) complaint is the seemingly inability to add multiple photos of an observation when first documenting it. The classic app, I was able to attach multiple photos before it uploaded. This one you seem to only be able to attach one picture, then after it uploads, you can add more. But that is so minor that it really doesn’t matter. This app is a permanent staple on my phone and exactly what I was looking for.

Edit: the adding multiple photos functionality is there, it just took me a bit to find it. It’s actually implemented in a very sleek way and I really like it.
reviews.title: It all started with a Shrew

reviews.text:
DARK MODE 
OPTIMIZED/USER FRIENDLY SEARCH 
GEOGRAPHIC SEARCH
ABILITY TO SCROLL THROUGH LISTINGS ALMOST LIKE TINDER WITH SWIPING
reviews.title: Dope lil app but needs work

reviews.text: Would often use iNaturalist to verify species. Uploading a photo is difficult because you can’t put in the photo details before uploading. You can’t select more than one photo at first either, just automatically starts analyzing the one you select. I have a nature social media account and would use iNaturalist to grab the correct scientific name but you can’t select and copy text anymore either. Glad the updated app is separate than the classic and can go back to that version.
reviews.title: Not as good as classic

reviews.text:
So far, I'm really liking this update! The UI is a lot less clunky and is much more responsive than the old one. It took a minute to figure out how to bring back the more advanced editing, although I was somewhat disappointed when I did. 

What I love:
- Batch upload of photos & sorting options
- Easier to navigate and much more intuitive UI
- Ability to save and come back to obs later without uploading them

What I'm missing from the old app:
- Ability to type in an address/coords for the location field (currently, only iNat Places show up and hitting enter/search does nothing if you try entering/searching for anything else)
- Adding obs to projects while uploading

What I would like to see that isn't in either mobile app:
- Batch editing/uploading obs with same time/location/etc.

Definitely a 5-star app if these things get addressed. It's already showing lots of progress!
reviews.title: Great But Lacking Features for Powerusers

reviews.text: Been a huge fan of the iNaturalist community for many years and absolutely loved Seek. While I’m a fan of the new app and the confidence ratings, it falls short of Seek’s UI. I enjoyed having a catalog of all my observations, which I could sort through by kingdom and class. The new list feels cluttered. Would also love to see some of the badge challenges come back. Still a phenomenal companion app and would recommend to anyone.
reviews.title: It’s not Seek… yet

reviews.text: I’ve used the previous iNat app for years, and I do like this new streamlined interface. However, it seems like the IDs of my observations are way more generalized than they used to be. Before I would get a usually pretty precise species ID, and now it just is usually “this is a flowering plant.” I also really miss being able to upload multiple photos of the same thing to one observation. TLDR; I like the new interface, but significant features from the original app seem to be lacking.
reviews.title: IDs Are Worse?

reviews.text: I have used the original iNat app for a few years now and I really like iNat Next too! While it took me a little bit to learn the new interface, I find myself using iNat Next more often than the old app. The one thing that bothers me is that often times, I am out in the field and unable to upload my observations immediately, so I keep them as drafts. What happens is that my photos for draft observations disappear, so I have to go back to my camera roll and reupload them to their right observations. When I’m logging many observations at a time, it gets frustrating. Other than that, I am an iNat Next enjoyer 🌱🌈💅
reviews.title: great app, just one bug that bothers me

reviews.text: The new iNat is just too much, and the way you upload an observation is awful. Rest in peace to people who like to add more than one photo for observations, you’ll have an extra two or three buttons now, and you’ll have to just always baby sit the settings to make sure you aren’t posting duplicates! And now on the iNat classic I no longer can look at my older observations, and I find it odd that they took away that feature around the same time this rolled out. I love iNat, but if there’s ever a competitor, I’m going to gladly give them a shot. RIP to iNaturalist 2025.
reviews.title: They are trying to do too much.

reviews.text: Haven’t used this new version much, yet,  but I’m not really happy with it, so far. I have a completed survey project, for which I am needing to submit my data. The “new” version has all of my captures scrambled, so I cannot find them in the chronological sequence in which they were shot. Thank goodness I kept the old version, so I can still find them, in order,  for my data.
reviews.title: Not Impressed Yet

reviews.text:
Tool tips and labeling icons is IMPORTANT!

The number one issue with the new app is the GUI, whoever made it is a technology native and put no effort into making sure that those less tech savvy can easily navigate. Those more used to a  windows based OS find the new GUI infuriatingly convoluted.

The ease of access to previously heavily used features is lacking. The new app GUI is confusing, poorly laid out and does not default to settings most users are familiar with. While more powerful, you can clearly see the launch has been disastrous. The “next” app should have been labeled “pro” since it gives easier access to high level tools and is genuinely more difficult to navigate. I think building from the interface of the first app should have been/currently be a major focus. Instead of what feels like starting from scratch in designing navigation.
reviews.title: GUI Improvements

reviews.text: A fun app and crisp looking update but no matter what i do it won’t give me notifications on my followers. So it’s useless for seeing what the folks you follow are doing.
reviews.title: Fun app but no follower updates

reviews.text: This app is only really useful for the social aspect of iNaturalist.
reviews.title: Use Classic Version Instead

reviews.text: This version is miles better than the classic. However, for the past few days on the following notifications tab, it has said “Something went wrong.” And I can’t see any of my notifications.
reviews.title: Love it! But lately….

reviews.text: This has completely revolutionized the way I interact with nature and see the world. There’s a steep learning curve to everything the app has to offer, but well worth trying it out.
reviews.title: Life changing

reviews.text: Why would you throttle my zoom? My phone can go 25x zoom and the app only lets me do 3x… yeah gonna get some great bird pics with that, thanks a lot. No no no it’s a great idea to put a governor on the zoom so we CANT use the full zoom on the device we paid for…. (???) effin what dude. Bunk.
reviews.title: Reducing my zoom? WHY

reviews.text: Why doesn’t this one have the explore on map feature like the classic app does? I will be using the old one until this changes.
reviews.title: No Map

reviews.text: Results aren’t that special or specific. For instance, on one plant, it just returns “dicot.” All flowering plants are either dicots or monocots, so - thanks for the vague answer! I can look at the leaves and see if the veins are parallel or not, I was looking for the name or at least species of plant.
reviews.title: Very Mid

reviews.text: Thank you.
reviews.title: Love the new update

reviews.text: I downloaded this app to keep up with my friends postings. While this app is great for that, it is not great for making my own. I will stick to classic for now.
reviews.title: Not as good as classic

reviews.text: The old version of the app is easier to navigate and more fun to use.
reviews.title: Less intuitive than older version

reviews.text: Really wish I could see other users’ photos when you click on a pin in the map view. You can do this in the old version and it’s both helpful and awesome to see exactly what others are finding in situ, rather than the generic wikipedia photo. AI isn’t inspiring any community here, either. While the suggestions may be “more” accurate, I looked forward to others input on my observations. Feel like they took a great app and removed all the fun/community…. more techy more boring.
reviews.title: Needs a lot of improvement

reviews.text: This looks & behaves like an app ported from iPhone, with limited use of the screen & an antiquated typing experience. I’d strongly prefer an app designed to take full advantage of my iPad Pro, including full use of the screen & the keyboard.
reviews.title: Please design a version for iPads

reviews.text: lame
reviews.title: Nothing but problems when uploading

reviews.text: I’m very happy to see some of Seek’s best features integrated into iNaturalist. I’ll use iNaturalist much more now, especially since the AI camera is soooo much faster compared to Seek. Overall, I think this is a great next step for the app and have enjoyed using it so far. I hope the developers will consider two additions. First, it would be helpful to be able to copy the coordinates from one observation to another in the app. It is super annoying to have to zoom in on a map while looking at a rural area without a lot of landmarks to find the right spot when I could just copy and paste from another nearby observation. The search function also didn’t bring up some towns in Colorado as an option so the location/map feature seems a bit incomplete/unwieldy. (I’m also not sure why some of my observations didn’t have a location when the observations a few minutes later did.) Second, it would be helpful to be able to crop photos in the app itself. Otherwise, thank you for these great updates!
reviews.title: Overall Great! Two Suggestions

reviews.text: I absolutely love the new app, especially where you can view the different species and how many observations of each species you’ve made making it easier so you do not duplicate your submissions which I have a bad habit of doing.
reviews.title: Awesome

reviews.text: While it was fun to learn the names of plants, bugs and animals, the app kept having errors and losing all of my observations. I uploaded them twice and added locations. Once I synced it, an error would occur and I’d lose everything. Such a bummer for my students who were excited to use the app.
reviews.title: Doesn’t Work

reviews.text: I’ve used iNat for a few years, mostly for my Bachelor’s degree, and I’ve always enjoyed it. However, with the new version I’ve had a few issues. The main issue being when I go to upload a picture, I can’t add a location. I have to upload the post and then go back and edit the photo to add a location. The other issue I have found is searching for a location. With the previous app, I was able to type in the coordinates in the map and was able to add the location that way. However, with this version I can’t get the map to load any location I type in, I have to manually zoom in and out on the map to get the place I need to add. I’ve tried various ways to fix this issue, including signing out and back in and then uninstalling the app and reinstalling it; neither have worked.
reviews.title: Good But Has Issues

reviews.text: I feel like in a lot of ways this new app is a better, or at least more user friendly of the old one. But why on earth would the map be removed? That was by far the most useful feature of the first app, I cannot fathom why it would have been removed.
reviews.title: Please being the map back

reviews.text: I usually don’t have a gripe with apps but I prefer the classic, the search function isn’t great if you want to find other observations in the area. You have to select a state if in the US, can’t even do a town/city search. It always backs out if you’re zoomed in and look for observations.. I don’t recommend this app till it’s polished like the classic version.
reviews.title: Not the best

reviews.text:
I’m so happy! Most of my pictures are on my phone and the old app worked nicely but was a bit limited. The new app has so many more features and is very similar to the website. I’m very pleased!

Two small bugs: if I go to suggest an ID on an audio clip, the whole app freezes. If I’m on the search menu to select an ID to select, sometimes when I tap on the result I want it will not accept the tap at all, or read it as if I tapped something else, even if I made double sure to wait til the menu was done loading. 

Very happy with the app, though, and it works very smoothly otherwise!
reviews.title: Exactly what I’ve been waiting for

reviews.text: Why create an entirely new app when you could have just updated the old version? Why the heck did you get rid of the map view on Explore? What a silly thing to get rid of. Not sure what the thought process was here but it wasn’t a good one.
reviews.title: Why an entirely new app?

reviews.text: new app feels like a bit of a downgrade
reviews.title: not really liking it

reviews.text: I uploaded my first observations using the new app and the time defaulted to the time of upload which is not when I observed the organisms uploaded. This can be problematic. Also, I filtered my map to my observations and they were showing up in completely different places on the map. Using the explore feature is too complex now. I have to tap a million things just to search for one thing. The way the map shows up drives me bonkers too. I want to see pins like the old app, not shaded squares. When I zoom in, I get pins but it’s too buggy. I am pleased with the notifications being better than the old app and that I can see my coords, but prefer to use the old app for everything else.
reviews.title: Buggy

reviews.text:
I can't recommend this app enough for learning about nature around you

The new UI update doubles the friction for making an observation though and that shouldn't have passed design review. (ie. For every photo I need to select it a second unnecessary time new grouping step)
reviews.title: Amazing but UI changes add friction

reviews.text: New app has a trendy UI that looks like a lot of popular apps. I’m not sure it makes the app more useful than the “Classic”. So far, it seems less useful to me. I’ll go back to the old app for now.
reviews.title: Trendy UI

reviews.text: Fast, intuitive and I love the new list of most commonly observed nearby flora and fauna
reviews.title: Excellent update

reviews.text: The new UI has some neat stuff but the classic is much better and I rly hope they don’t take down classic in favor of this
reviews.title: Still love iNat but classic is much better

reviews.text: The new App is really nice, but it try’s to identify surrounding plants when I’m trying to make an observation of a bird or insect. So you can still find the observation Map, but it’s more difficult to use. It starts on a a graph system that I don’t really care about. I’d rather have the original location pins like before. You can get to where it shows you the pins but only if you’re super zoomed in on the map, and it’s not even color coded like the original one. BRING BACK THE COLORED MARKERS!!! I miss it, and sure a lot of others miss it to. Also make it more easily findable for others! Still love you guys, and will continue using the app, but my rating won’t change until the user friendly features are brought back!
reviews.title: It’s nice, but they should’ve kept some of the older features.

reviews.text: The new app is slick, elegant and maintains the technical details. Highly recommend.
reviews.title: Absolutely fantastic update

reviews.text: I love iNat. Long time user on android. A few years ago I switched to an iPhone and the iOS version was so bad. It lacked the key features I wanted in the field. Often I would consider switchback to an android just to used the better android version of the app. This is a MUCH improved iOS app. Thank! Long awaited and happy it’s finally here!
reviews.title: Much improve iPhone version

reviews.text:
crashed my phone twice, aggressively asks for photo permissions to the point where this interferes with the user experience.

excited for AI capabilities in identifying wildlife. this app is currently not there. features were unable to be accessed without the app needing to take a real breather.
reviews.title: next does not mean the app is better

reviews.text: As an iNat user since 2020 I was quite excited to see this app, it has great potential but every update is making the app exponentially we’re. The user interface is more confusing, not allowing batch importations anymore, and the heavy reliance on CV. Had to delete it and go back to the old app.
reviews.title: Downhill

reviews.text:
It’s good. I’ve used the other app for years now so I will have to get used to this new user interface. 

Would be really nice if you could video observations in the expanded map of showing observations within an organisms description page (the one with taxonomy, Wikipedia and map). Please please add this. Thanks
reviews.title: It’s good

reviews.text: Only improvements I would like to see are the seasonality charts and making it easier to find people you aren’t already following.
reviews.title: Deserved upgrade to iNat

reviews.text: iNat Next offers a major advance and intuitive interface to connect people with Nature.  I’ve been a member of the iNat community for over a decade and I appreciate the continued effort to make real-time uploads and identifications easier and more reliable with every release and every new training data set.
reviews.title: Connecting to Nature

reviews.text: Significant downgrade from the old app. Doesn't open automatically to nearby observations. Observation pinpoint don’t differ in color between taxa.
reviews.title: Old app was better

reviews.text:
I’ve used the old app for a long time now and i do not like the new one. When you upload a photo it automatically suggests something and doesn’t have the same option to put in what you know it is, you can only scroll through suggestions. If you have a photo of a bird in a tree it will only pick up the tree and you’re screwed :/
The old one is great, i never had any issues. It feels like you switched to the “modern” designs apps have now where they do everything for you and there is no customization/less choices. Genuinely if you guys take down the old app I will exclusively use the desktop version, this isn’t unusable it’s just aggressively mediocre
reviews.title: Don’t fix what isn’t broken

reviews.text: I want to give this app a higher rating because of how cool, nifty and neat it is, but…the main reason for my neutral review is due to the fact that I currently can’t view my followers or those who I follow through the iOS app. It’s been over 6 years that I haven’t used my account…so there might be issues with it. 🤷🏻‍♂️ I believe I already changed my outdated password, if not, I’ll give another go. ✌🏼
reviews.title: Buggy app (pun not intended)

reviews.text:
iNat has entirely changed my life and how I approach and understand nature. This app is an essential tool I use whenever I’m out in the field and I need to identify an unfamiliar species of plant or insect. All photos submitted via the app contribute to the world’s largest crowdsourced dataset of biodiversity, supporting countless projects in wildlife biology, conservation science and ecology. And what’s best, the app is entirely open source. 

The new iOS app could still use some UX improvements but it’s vastly superior to the previous one in terms of functionality and is getting closer to feature parity with the website. Keep up the great work!
reviews.title: The most essential app on my phone

reviews.text: This app does have more functionality than the old one, but its interface is unintuitive and  visually clunky. The lack of any onboarding tutorial and the official launch being so close to the City Nature Challenge makes me fear the sort of user mistakes I’ll have to deal with as an identifier. iNat Next feels unpolished and should not have been fully launched so soon.
reviews.title: Unpolished

reviews.text: Much easier to use with a crisp and clean interface and ability to ignore login reminders if I’m not using my account. My major complaint with it is that the UI and species names take up the entirety of the image, rendering the whole app a little useless. Fewer images are displayed (4 instead of the old version’s 12 on my device) which wouldn’t be so bad if one could actually see the plant or animal in question. I hope this gets fixed, otherwise I may not be using the app much.
reviews.title: Clean but Bulky UI

reviews.text: I appreciate the updated design and think it will attract a broader audience than the original app.
reviews.title: Better Updated UI

reviews.text: jumping in here immediately from the old inat app + web browser user to upload a picture of a bird i saw in a tree today and i'm immediately swarmed with different options for the tree's ID. I'm sure that it was plenty accurate if i was looking to ID the tree but i just wasn't! i could go back and finagle the ID but i did have to save it with a tree ID first. just a bit too much extra messing around if i wanted to upload something quickly. I do like the new UI changes otherwise though
reviews.title: overwhelming auto-suggest

reviews.text: I’ve been a dedicated iNaturalist user since 2018, and it is the one app I can’t live without. iNaturalist Next has really taken it to a whole new level! Love being able to see whether I’m getting a good read on a plant before I even take the picture. It takes a little getting used to, but overall I’m living it.
reviews.title: Makes the best nature app out there even better!

reviews.text: this app feels less intuitive to use than the original app. the ui seems more cluttered. when i try and post an observation for some reason it tried posting two observations instead of one and then crashed. also i don’t like how you have to wait for one observation to upload in order to upload more observations, it just takes too much time. can someone tell me what features besides ai this app adds over the other one?
reviews.title: worse than the original

reviews.text:
As a long time iNaturalist user, the desktop version was always far superior than the iPhone app. After trying out the new and improved interface, I almost like the app better now! Thank you so so much to the developers and behind the scenes people who were able to make this happen. The science community thanks you.
The only thing I’d love to see is a Projects tab on the bulk upload page/observation edit page, so I can add my observations to my projects as I upload them instead of retroactively online!
reviews.title: best nature ID and science app!

reviews.text: It's finally a complete app with nowadays UI and usability!
reviews.title: Awesome upgrade of iNaturalist!

reviews.text: im soo glad this app exists the old app was sooo bad it was nearly impossible to use and the ability to see the taxon now in the app is great i always used the web version instead of the old app super convenient
reviews.title: breath of fresh air

reviews.text: I’ve been using Seek and iNaturalist in tandem since they came out.  As a wildlands manager, ethnobotanist, and backpacker I continue to try every species ID app out there and these have been far and away the best (though Cornell’s Merlin app combined with eBird is great for bird sound ID). This app takes it up a level by essentially combining the two.  I can’t express how impressed I’ve been with the developers continue improvement over the years, really listening to the advice people offer, and I just want to express my gratitude. Thank You
reviews.title: This is my dream app

reviews.text:
As of Fall 2024, the developers are still working on the App,  it I think this is a great improvement over the previous version. 
The app is also a wonderful gateway to resources that help us learn about the living things around us, contribute to science, and connect to a community of people invested in spreading and sharing knowledge about biodiversity.
reviews.title: Significant improvement
